mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 290, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This dual condition signals sustained headwinds to S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-11-15, FEDFUNDS=3.78% and CPIAUCSL=298.349, both elevated
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between the interaction term and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns. This is an always-on trend signal requiring no threshold activation.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between 3-month PAYEMS change and 60-day forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window, or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in 50% of months with declining payroll employment.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1400 thousand units annualized, it signals weakening residential construction demand and reduced forward economic growth expectations. This threshold breach predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1425.0 thousand as of 2022-11-15, near threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when HOUST < 1400, or Rank IC between HOUST and 5-day forward returns exceeds -0.02 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month when supply-side constraints tighten or demand weakens. This deceleration signals reduced corporate capacity utilization and earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth rate and 20-day forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window, or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in 50% of months with sub-0.5% INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly measure consumer and residential demand. When both decline simultaneously, it signals broad-based demand destruction that compresses equity earnings multiples. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0% AND HOUST month-over-month growth < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 689443.0 million and HOUST at 1425.0 thousand as of 2022-11-15
failure_condition: The interaction term (joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST) produces Rank IC with 60-day forward returns below 0.08 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when both series decline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.0%, it signals labor market slack that reduces wage pressure and inflation expectations. Markets reprrice Fed policy expectations toward eventual easing, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as discount rates compress.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% as of 2022-11-15, below threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with labor market softening
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE and 20-day forward SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 1.5% annualized while the federal funds rate remains above 3.0%, creating a regime where monetary policy is restrictive relative to growth. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as markets price recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20039.406 billion (2017 dollars) as of 2022-10-15, FEDFUNDS at 3.78% as of 2022-11-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with slowing growth
failure_condition: The interaction term (slow growth + high rates) produces Rank IC with 60-day forward returns below 0.07 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 1.0% month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows above 0.3% month-over-month, signaling synchronized supply and labor expansion. This dual expansion produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice earnings growth upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 1.0% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.5175 and PAYEMS at 153548.0 thousand as of 2022-11-15
failure_condition: The interaction term (strong industrial and labor growth) produces Rank IC with 5-day forward returns below 0.06 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in more than 50% of instances when both conditions are met.
